content: agent1: implementing 'Add Token Position Tracking' feature - adding return_positions param to encode() in tiktoken/core.py. Will touch core.py's Encoding.encode and possibly encode_ordinary. Let me know if you're touching the same file.

agent1: ack. I'm adding 'return_positions: bool = False' as a keyword-only param on encode(), placed after disallowed_special (so signature order will be: allowed_special, disallowed_special, return_positions). Implementation: after computing 'tokens' list (no change to existing try/except body other than assigning to a local var instead of early-returning), I append 'if not return_positions: return tokens' then compute positions via a new helper self._token_positions(tokens) and 'return tokens, positions'. I'm about to save this edit now (~2 min). If you add 'compress' too, suggest signature order: allowed_special, disallowed_special, return_positions, compress - and apply compress to 'tokens' BEFORE position computation so positions still make sense (or exclude compress+return_positions combo). I'll ping you when done with the exact diff.

agent1: done. My encode() diff: added 'return_positions: bool = False' as the last kwarg (after disallowed_special), changed the two 'return self._core_bpe.encode(...)' lines inside try/except to 'tokens = ...', then at the end: 'if not return_positions: return tokens' / 'return tokens, self._token_positions(tokens)'. I added a new private helper '_token_positions' (near _encode_bytes) and refactored decode_with_offsets to reuse it - purely internal, its public signature/behavior is unchanged. For your compress param: since I only append after the try/except (right before returning), you can safely apply compress to 'tokens' right after the try/except block and before my 'if not return_positions' check - that way compress+return_positions compose naturally (positions computed on the compressed list). Suggested final kwarg order: allowed_special, disallowed_special, return_positions, compress. Go ahead and edit, this should merge cleanly since we're touching non-overlapping lines within the function body.
